name: Republic of Iraq
note: General Abd al-Karim Qasim's 1958 coup killed the royal family and created the republic. Ba'ath Party coups (1963, 1968) brought Saddam Hussein to power (formally 1979). Iran-Iraq War (1980–88); Kuwait invasion (1990); Gulf War; UN sanctions devastated the country. US invasion (2003) destroyed the state; Sunni insurgency, al-Qaeda, and ISIS emerged from the chaos. The 2005 constitution and Shia-dominated democracy have been unable to overcome sectarian divisions.

figures.name: Saddam Hussein
figures.significance: Ba'athist dictator 1979–2003; used chemical weapons on Kurds (Halabja 1988); invaded Iran then Kuwait; executed after US invasion — his fall unleashed sectarian war that produced ISIS

figures.name: Ayatollah Sistani
figures.significance: Grand Ayatollah; Iraq's most authoritative Shia cleric; his fatwas mobilising Shia volunteers against ISIS (2014) saved Iraq from collapse; consistent advocate for democracy within Islamic framework
